principles.label: Trust Your Instinct Above All Else
principles.summary: Your inner voice is an emotional GPS system. Every good decision comes from being attuned to what feels like the next right move, while trouble arises from overriding that instinct with rationalization.
principles.details: Listening to your inner voice leads to right decisions, while ignoring it inevitably leads to mistakes. If something feels unnatural or wrong, it is a signal to pivot.
principles.representative_quote: Every right decision I’ve made—every right decision I’ve ever made—has come from my gut. And every wrong decision I’ve ever made was a result of me not listening to the greater voice of myself.

principles.label: Transform Pain Into Personal Growth
principles.summary: Adversity, failures, and past hurts should be used as learning experiences to gain insight and evolve into the fullest expression of yourself.
principles.details: While winning is enjoyable, the moments of uncertainty and failure are when you actually learn the most about yourself. Every experience, especially the painful ones, is an opportunity to learn something about life.
principles.representative_quote: All of my experiences, even the painful ones, have been there to teach me something about life.

principles.label: The Universal Need for Validation
principles.summary: Regardless of background or status, every person fundamentally wants to know that they are seen, heard, and that their words matter.
principles.details: After interviewing tens of thousands of people, Oprah realized that all human arguments and interactions boil down to the same core desire for connection and validation.
principles.representative_quote: all of your arguments are really about the same thing. It’s about: Did you hear me? Did you see me? And did what I said mean anything to you?

principles.label: Intention Determines the Outcome
principles.summary: The intention behind an action is what propels it, and therefore dictates the result or consequence.
principles.details: Based on the laws of physics, every action has an equal and opposite reaction. You should not undertake any project or action without being fully clear about why you intend to do it.
principles.representative_quote: I don’t do anything, without being fully clear, about why I intend to do it. Because the intention is going to determine the reaction, the result, or the consequence in every circumstance.

principles.label: Personal Responsibility for Your Reality
principles.summary: You are entirely responsible for your own life and create your reality through your thoughts and actions.
principles.details: Blaming circumstances, parents, or background strips you of your power. Recognizing that you are defined by your possibilities rather than your past allows you to achieve anything.
principles.representative_quote: What I learned at a very early age was that I was responsible for my life. And as I became more spiritually conscious, I learned that we all are responsible for ourselves, that you create your own reality by the way you think and therefore act.

principles.label: Service as a Way of Life
principles.summary: True happiness and fulfillment require serving something larger than yourself.
principles.details: Service isn't limited to specific charitable jobs; it's about how you act and interact with others daily. Life is a reciprocal exchange, meaning that in order to move forward, you must give back.
principles.representative_quote: This is what I know for sure: In order to be truly happy, you must live along with and you have to stand for something larger than yourself.

principles.label: Authenticity and Self-Love
principles.summary: The ultimate goal of life is to become more of yourself, seeing your worth through your own eyes rather than relying on external validation.
principles.details: Relying on others' perceptions leaves you vulnerable to their insecurities. A life of substance is built on knowing and standing for your personal truth.
principles.representative_quote: A lesson you will have to learn again and again: to see yourself with your own eyes, to love yourself from your own heart.

principles.label: Align Personality with Purpose
principles.summary: When you take your natural skills and passions and align them with the deeper potential of your soul's calling, you achieve authentic empowerment.
principles.details: This alignment makes you unstoppable and ensures you wake up every day fired up about your work.
principles.representative_quote: If you align your personality with your purpose and nobody can touch you and you wake up everyday and you are fired up.

principles.label: Obesity is a Biological Disease
principles.summary: Obesity is driven by biology and a bodily 'set point,' not a failure of willpower or a lack of discipline.
principles.details: Understanding obesity as a disease removes the deep personal shame associated with weight struggles and allows individuals to utilize medical tools (like GLP-1 medications) responsibly to manage the chronic condition.
principles.representative_quote: it's just biology. It had nothing to do with how much willpower or my willingness to continue to work out and starve myself.

principles.label: Cultivate Gratitude
principles.summary: Appreciating what you have leads to abundance, whereas focusing on lack ensures you will never feel satisfied.
principles.details: Gratitude shifts your mindset from scarcity to abundance, multiplying the value of what is already present in your life.
principles.representative_quote: Be thankful for what you have; you'll end up having more. If you concentrate on what you don't have, you will never, ever have enough.

principles.label: The Law of Attraction is a Tool, Not Magic
principles.summary: Positive thinking is valuable for clarifying goals and focusing actions, but it is not a substitute for practical action or medical science.
principles.details: The Law of Attraction should be used alongside established treatments and practical steps, not as a replacement for them or as a magical explanation for all tragedies.
principles.representative_quote: The law of attraction is a tool that can help you decide what you want your life to be, and then begin to help you focus on making the best choices through action to create that life.

frameworks.label: The "Get Still" Decision Process
frameworks.summary: A method for making decisions when you are uncertain and tempted to crowdsource opinions.
frameworks.details: Steps: 1. Recognize that asking others means you don't know the answer yourself. 2. Stop asking for external opinions. 3. Do nothing. 4. Get still and wait until the answer naturally comes to you from your inner voice.
frameworks.representative_quote: when you don't know what to do you do nothing you get still until you do know

frameworks.label: Intention-Based Decision Making
frameworks.summary: A filter for evaluating new projects, pitches, or business opportunities to ensure they align with your ultimate purpose.
frameworks.details: Steps: 1. Require the proposing party to fully think out and state their intention for the project. 2. Evaluate the proposal based strictly on that stated intention. 3. Decide if the intention aligns with how you want to impact the world.
frameworks.representative_quote: Do not bring me a show, unless you have fully thought out what is your intention for doing it.

mental_models.label: Earth as a School
mental_models.summary: A lens for viewing life where the world is a school and our lives are the classrooms. Negative experiences are reframed as curriculum designed for personal evolution.
mental_models.details: When facing a detour, roadblock, or full-blown crisis, view it as a lesson dressed up in disguise by the 'grandest university of all' to help you grow.
mental_models.representative_quote: I consider the world, this Earth, to be like a school and our life the classrooms.

mental_models.label: Emotional GPS System
mental_models.summary: Viewing one's inner voice and instincts as a navigational tool designed to guide them toward their supreme moment of destiny and away from danger.
mental_models.details: If a situation feels like an 'unnatural act,' it is your internal GPS telling you to change course, regardless of the logical or financial incentives to stay.
mental_models.representative_quote: All of my best decisions in life have come because I was attuned to what really felt like the next right move for me.

mental_models.label: The Third Law of Motion (Karma/Intention)
mental_models.summary: Applying physics (every action has an equal and opposite reaction) to human behavior and decision-making.
mental_models.details: Thoughts and intentions are actions that will inevitably return to the sender in a circular motion. The intention dictates the consequence.
mental_models.representative_quote: Because the intention is going to determine the reaction, the result, or the consequence in every circumstance.

mental_models.label: Luck as Preparation Meeting Opportunity
mental_models.summary: Reframing 'luck' not as random chance, but as the exact moment when a person's hard work and preparation intersect with a favorable opportunity.
mental_models.details: Success is attributed to grace, blessings, and being prepared to handle the moments of opportunity that arise.
mental_models.representative_quote: Nothing about my life is lucky. Nothing. A lot of grace, a lot of blessings, a lot of divine order, but I don’t believe in luck. For me, luck is the moment of preparation meeting the moment of opportunity.

mental_models.label: Failure as Redirection
mental_models.summary: Viewing failure not as a negative end-state, but as a divine or natural signal pointing you toward a different, better path.
mental_models.details: When facing setbacks, look at them as the universe's way of pointing you in a new direction.
mental_models.representative_quote: I don’t believe in failure. It’s not failure if you enjoyed the process.

mental_models.label: The Full Cup
mental_models.summary: Reclaiming the phrase 'full of yourself' as a positive necessity. You must nurture your own spirit so that your 'cup' overflows, allowing you to give to others from the excess.
mental_models.details: Having a daily spiritual practice feeds your own spirit so you don't burn out and have enough energy to offer others.
mental_models.representative_quote: You want your cup to be so full it's running over so you have enough to offer others.

mental_models.label: Holding Your Breath Underwater
mental_models.summary: A metaphor for relying purely on willpower to fight a biological set point.
mental_models.details: You can force yourself to stay submerged for a while through extreme effort (like extreme dieting), but eventually, biology will force you to the surface.
mental_models.representative_quote: That the body was just trying to do what the body does.

mental_models.label: Standing as 10,000
mental_models.summary: A visualization technique used to overcome intimidation or isolation by imagining oneself entering a room backed by the energy and heritage of thousands of ancestors.
mental_models.details: Useful when walking into spaces where you are the minority or feel out of place, allowing you to feel empowered rather than isolated.

heuristics.label: Get Still When Uncertain
heuristics.summary: When you don't know what to do, do nothing. Get still until the answer comes.
heuristics.representative_quote: When you don't know what to do, you do nothing. You get still until you do know.

heuristics.label: When You Know Better, Do Better
heuristics.summary: Allow yourself to change your mind and actions when presented with new information or understanding.
heuristics.representative_quote: as when Maya Angelou used to teach, when you know better, you do better.

heuristics.label: Take the Next Right Step
heuristics.summary: To move forward through failure or uncertainty, focus only on taking the immediate next right step.
heuristics.representative_quote: In order to move forward you move forward by taking the next right step.

heuristics.label: Look for the Lesson
heuristics.summary: Actively search for the underlying lesson in every experience, especially the difficult ones.
heuristics.representative_quote: Look for the lesson in almost everything that you do and every experience.

heuristics.label: You Can Have It All, Just Not All At Once
heuristics.summary: Pace your ambitions and recognize that life happens in seasons.
heuristics.representative_quote: You can have it all. Just not all at once.

signature_quotes.label: Turn Wounds Into Wisdom
signature_quotes.summary: A succinct summary of her philosophy on overcoming adversity.

signature_quotes.label: The Universal Question
signature_quotes.summary: Her realization about the core of human interaction.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Did you hear me? Did you see me? And did what I said mean anything to you? That’s what everything’s about.

signature_quotes.label: Knowing What You Don't Want
signature_quotes.summary: Advice on finding your path when you feel lost.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Knowing what you don’t want to do is the best possible place to be if you don’t know what to do.

signature_quotes.label: You Are Your Possibilities
signature_quotes.summary: A powerful statement on personal responsibility and overcoming background.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: You cannot blame apartheid, your parents, your circumstances, because you are not your circumstances. You are your possibilities.

signature_quotes.label: Gratitude and Abundance
signature_quotes.summary: Her core belief on the power of gratitude.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Be thankful for what you have; you'll end up having more. If you concentrate on what you don't have, you will never, ever have enough.

signature_quotes.label: Self-Love
signature_quotes.summary: Advice to her younger self on self-worth.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: A lesson you will have to learn again and again: to see yourself with your own eyes, to love yourself from your own heart.

signature_quotes.label: Getting Still
signature_quotes.summary: Her ultimate advice for decision making.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: When you don't know what to do, you do nothing. You get still until you do know.

anti_patterns.label: Overriding Instinct with Rationalization
anti_patterns.summary: Trying to convince yourself to do something for logical reasons (like money or prestige) when your inner voice says no.
anti_patterns.details: This leads to trouble and takes you off your true path. Every wrong decision comes from ignoring the greater voice of yourself.

anti_patterns.label: Asking Everyone Else for Advice
anti_patterns.summary: Crowdsourcing your life decisions instead of listening to yourself.
anti_patterns.details: It indicates you don't really know the answer yourself; you are looking externally instead of getting still and listening to your own truth.

anti_patterns.label: Blaming Circumstances
anti_patterns.summary: Blaming your circumstances, parents, or background for your current situation in life.
anti_patterns.details: It prevents you from taking responsibility for your own reality and blinds you to your possibilities.

anti_patterns.label: Believing Weight Loss is Purely Willpower
anti_patterns.summary: Ignoring the biological reality of 'set points' and treating obesity as a moral failing.
anti_patterns.details: People can use extreme discipline temporarily, but biology will eventually drive the body back to its baseline, leading to inevitable regain and deep personal shame.

anti_patterns.label: Treating the Law of Attraction as Magic
anti_patterns.summary: Using positive thinking as a get-rich-quick scheme or a magical cure for all tragedies and illnesses.
anti_patterns.details: It ignores the necessity of practical action and established medical science, reducing a complex reality to a single, oversimplified law.

anti_patterns.label: Concentrating on What You Lack
anti_patterns.summary: Focusing your energy on what you don't have rather than what you do.
anti_patterns.details: It creates a mindset of scarcity where you will never, ever feel like you have enough.
